input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't in .NET 10.

This is a known change. In .NET 10 (and actually starting with Android 11+), `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in the Android platform itself. .NET MAUI has moved toward using `WindowInsets` and edge-to-edge display handling.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer.

In .NET MAUI for .NET 10 targeting Android, the `Resize` option for `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust` was deprecated at the Android platform level (since Android R / API 30). The recommended approach is:


- Use `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Pan` to shift the window up when the keyboard appears, or handle keyboard insets using the modern Android `WindowInsets` API
- For .NET MAUI, wrapping the input in a `ScrollView` or adjusting padding dynamically based on keyboard visibility typically resolves the issue, since `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` no longer works as it did previously

The most straightforward approach is setting `AdjustPan` via the `WindowSoftInputMode` attribute in the Android activity, though if the layout needs to actually resize rather than pan, I'd need to listen for `WindowInsets` changes and apply bottom padding when the IME becomes visible.
